Sets a property on a fresh AA to the return value of a function call that constructs a new object.
Passes a fresh AA into a helper function that sets a property on it internally.
Sets a fresh AA onto `m`, then calls a helper function that sets a property on it.
Passes a pre-existing (setup-created) AA into a helper function that sets a property on it internally.
Sets a property on a pre-existing AA to the return value of a function call that constructs a new object.
Calls a helper function that sets a property on a pre-existing AA stored on `m`.
Writes `invalid` into 11 scattered indices of a pre-sized `roArray`, creating a sparse array.
Writes `invalid` into an associative array keyed by stringified numeric indices, simulating sparse-array behavior with an AA.
Splits a short 3-item comma-delimited string using `.tokenize()` then converts the result to an array.
Splits a short 3-item comma-delimited string using `.Split()`.
Splits a medium 50-item comma-delimited string using `.tokenize()` then converts to an array.
Splits a medium 50-item comma-delimited string using `.Split()`.
Splits a long 100-item comma-delimited string using `.tokenize()` then converts to an array.
Splits a long 100-item comma-delimited string using `.Split()`.
Calls `.startsWith()` on small/medium/large strings for a prefix that is present.
Calls `.startsWith()` on small/medium/large strings for a prefix that is absent.
Uses `instr()` to check that a substring occurs at position 1 of small/medium/large strings (hit case).
Uses `instr()` to check a near-start substring's position against 1, on small/medium/large strings (miss case).
Uses `instr()` to check a substring located near the end against position 1 of small/medium/large strings (miss case).
Reads an AA value using a literal (hyphenated) string key via bracket notation.
Reads an AA value using a key built at runtime by concatenating a variable with a literal suffix, via bracket notation.
Reads a nested AA value by chaining two bracket-notation lookups (AA within AA).
Assigns a single precomputed 26-word string literal to a variable (baseline, no concatenation).
Builds a 26-word string by chaining the `+` operator across 26 string literals.
Builds a 26-word string by calling `.Join("")` on a 26-element array literal of words.
Builds a 26-word string by pushing 26 words one at a time onto an array, then calling `.Join("")`.
Same push-then-`.Join("")` pattern as the previous test (duplicate implementation/name in the same suite).
Builds a 26-word string using a single `"%s"` x26 format string with 26 arguments.
Builds the 26-word string by repeatedly calling `.format()` in chunks of ~5 args, chaining results together.
Builds the 26-word string using bslib's `.arg()` substitution method in chunks of 6, combining the partial results.
Builds the 26-word string via a custom `stringJoin()` helper that branches to the correct fixed-arity `.format()` call based on arg count.
Same as stringJoin but using a cached AA of arity-specific formatter functions instead of an if/else branch tree.
Builds the 26-word string by repeatedly calling a `substitute()` helper with `"{1}{2}{3}"`-style placeholders, two new words at a time.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 5-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 10-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 15-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 20-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 40-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to a 60-character value.
Sets a roSGNode string field to an 80-character value.
Calls `type()` once on a large string variable.
Calls `type()` twice in a row on a large string variable.
Calls `type()` three times in a row on a large string variable.
Calls `type()` four times in a row on a large string variable.
Calls `type()` five times in a row on a large string variable.
Checks whether a long string is a String type using `type()` called once (cached) and compared against "String"/"roString".
Checks the same String type equality, calling `type()` fresh for each of two comparisons.
Checks a long string's type by calling `type()` fresh inside an `instr()` search for "String".
Checks a long string's type by calling `type()` once (cached) then searching the cached value with `instr()` for "String".
Checks whether a boxed (auto-boxed roString) long string is a String type using `type()` called once and compared for equality.

testName: boxed type call each time - equality
operation: Checks whether a boxed long string is a String type, calling `type()` fresh for each equality comparison.

testName: boxed type call each time - instr
operation: Checks a boxed long string's type via `instr()` search using a fresh `type()` call.
Checks a boxed long string's type via `instr()` search using a cached `type()` call.
Retrieves a roSGNode's component subtype name via `.subtype()`.
Reads a custom Boolean field that was explicitly added to a roSGNode.
Reads the built-in `focusable` field that already exists on the roSGNode's base type.
Computes elapsed milliseconds using a freshly-created `roDateTime`'s `AsSeconds()`/`GetMilliseconds()`.
Computes elapsed milliseconds using a freshly-created `roTimeSpan`'s `TotalMilliseconds()`.
Selects between two Boolean values using a standard if/else block.
Selects between two Boolean values using a `ternary()` helper function (mimicking BrighterScript's ternary operator).
Selects between two Boolean values using an immediately-invoked anonymous function containing an if/else.
Calls `.threadInfo()` on a roSGNode fresh on every iteration to get thread metadata.
Reads a previously-captured `threadInfo()` result stored as a field on another roSGNode (an implicit AA clone), avoiding a fresh call.
Performs an integer division (1/1) with no error handling, as a baseline.
Performs an integer division (1/1) inside a try/catch block where no exception is thrown.
Performs a divide-by-zero (1/0) inside a try/catch block, triggering and catching a runtime exception.
Explicitly throws a custom string error message inside a try/catch block and catches it.
Reads an existing nested property off `m` with no try/catch wrapper.
Reads an existing nested property off `m` inside a try/catch block where no exception occurs.
Attempts to read a property off a nonexistent nested path on `m` inside a try/catch block, triggering and catching a runtime error.
Mutates each element of a ~500-item array in a for loop with no error handling.
Mutates each element of a ~500-item array in a for loop, wrapping each individual iteration's mutation in its own try/catch.
Mutates each element of a ~500-item array in a for loop, wrapping the entire loop in a single try/catch block.
Writes a value (number/short string/long string variant) to a typed field on a roSGNode.
Writes a value to a property on `m`.
Writes a value to a plain local variable.
Reads a value from a typed field on a roSGNode.
Reads a value from a property on `m`.
Reads a value from a plain local variable.
Calls `.GetVersion()` on a `roAppInfo` instance created once in setup and reused.
Creates a fresh `roAppInfo` instance inside the test body, then calls `.GetVersion()` on it.
Creates a fresh `roAppInfo` instance and calls `.GetVersion()` on it in a single chained expression.
Calls `.getModel()` on a `roDeviceInfo` instance created once in setup and reused.
Creates a fresh `roDeviceInfo` instance inside the test body, then calls `.getModel()` on it.
Creates a fresh `roDeviceInfo` instance and calls `.getModel()` on it in a single chained expression.
Calls `.IsMatch()` on a pre-created `roRegex` instance (email pattern) reused across calls.
Creates a fresh `roRegex` instance (email pattern) inside the test body, then calls `.IsMatch()` on it.

testName: untyped small list of small strings
operation: Assigns a 6-element array of short strings to an untyped `array` field on a roSGNode.

testName: typed small list of small strings
operation: Assigns a 6-element array of short strings to a `stringarray`-typed field on a roSGNode.
